name: Skilled trades & semiconductor engineers
severityNote: Electrician + construction shortage gates every site
description: Datacenter and fab construction is gated by an undersupply of licensed electricians, HVAC technicians, pipefitters, and millwrights, plus a parallel shortage of process engineers for advanced-node fab ramps. The labor constraint is structural — apprenticeship pipelines were thin in the 2010s and cannot be rebuilt on AI-cycle timelines.
stackPosition: Apprenticeship + journeyman pipeline (IBEW, NECA, Joint ATEPs) → certified trades labor pool → EPC contractor allocation (Bechtel, Fluor, Holder, MWH) → datacenter / fab site → commissioning crew → ops handoff. Engineering staffing for fabs follows a parallel path: undergrad EE/CompE → industrial-internship pipeline → process engineering → senior expert IC. Both pipelines are saturated by current AI-cycle demand.

supplyStructure.name: AECOM / Jacobs / Fluor
supplyStructure.note: Engineering + management; labor sourced from union halls

supplyStructure.name: Independent / regional
supplyStructure.note: Long tail of regional electrical and mechanical contractors

indicators.name: BLS construction employment + JOLTS openings, electrical trade SOC codes
Quanta Services + EMCOR + Comfort Systems backlog and book-to-bill
Major hyperscaler datacenter capex commentary on labor availability

companies.name: Quanta Services
companies.note: Largest US specialty electrical contractor; datacenter + grid exposure

companies.name: MasTec
companies.note: Industrial + power generation EPC; less pure datacenter mix

companies.name: EMCOR Group
companies.note: Mechanical + electrical contractor; high datacenter mix

companies.name: Comfort Systems USA
companies.note: Pure-play mechanical contractor; modular construction beneficiary

companies.name: AECOM
companies.note: Engineering + program management; fewer billable hours per project

companies.name: Construction Partners / Sterling Infrastructure
companies.note: Site work, pad prep — earlier in project lifecycle

insight: Skilled-trade contractors are the under-appreciated way to play the AI capex cycle — they are paid a premium for execution on a constrained input, and their backlog visibility now extends 3-5 years. EMCOR and Comfort Systems are the cleanest expressions: mechanical contractors with datacenter mix above 25% and accelerating. The widow-maker trade is academic — universities cannot scale EE/CompE grads on the relevant timescale, so engineering labor inflation is a permanent cost embedded in every new node. The asymmetric kicker is non-union states with H-1B-friendly counties: Phoenix, Austin, Columbus where labor supply elasticity is highest.

debates.question: Does electrician wage inflation peak in 2026 or run into 2027-2028?
debates.currentBelief: Runs through 2027 as concurrent fab + datacenter builds compound; 2028 normalization probable

debates.question: Do hyperscalers materially shift to modular / prefab construction by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Yes for Microsoft and Meta; Amazon slower; Google ambivalent
